_comment: OC Pledge example — research preregistration. A researcher commits PUBLICLY to the exact bytes of a future preprint without revealing the content. At the deadline, publishing an OC Stamp envelope of the pre-committed hash automatically resolves the pledge to KEPT (deterministic; no signature needed at the pledge-outcome layer). If the deadline passes without a matching stamp, a verifier publishes a deterministic outcome envelope with `outcome: broken` and the pledge attaches as broken to bc1qbob's public ledger forever. To run this example: (1) compute SHA-256 of your draft, (2) replace the content_hash in proposition + resolution.query, (3) BIP-322-sign the canonical message in your wallet, (4) publish the envelope to Nostr kind-30078 with d-tag oc-pledge:<id>. The pledge_id below is reproducible — re-canonicalize the inputs and rehash to verify.
_flow: PROTOCOL.md Flow 1 — Alice preregisters a research finding (chain_state + stamp_published)
_resolves_to: deterministic kept on stamp publication, deterministic broken on absence
proposition: I will publish a research preprint with content_hash sha256:e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855 before 2026-09-01.

sig.value: <base64 BIP-322 signature by bc1qbob over hex pledge_id>
